response:
Nurse Emily organizes a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility, ensuring that each resident has the opportunity to participate. She divides the 24 residents into groups of 6, resulting in 4 groups in total. To support the creative process, Emily prepares 3 writing prompts per group for the session. This means she needs 3 prompts × 4 groups = 12 initial writing prompts. 

In addition to the initial prompts, Emily has prepared an extra set of 10 prompts to be shared equally among all the groups. These prompts are intended to provide further engagement if the groups finish their initial set. 

Emily also maintains a library of writing resources, which includes a variety of prompts and writing exercises. This library helps her quickly access new ideas when needed. She also encourages the use of the facility’s library for additional inspiration, ensuring that the residents have access to a wide range of creative materials. 

In total, Emily needs to have 12 + 10 = 22 writing prompts prepared for the session, including both the initial and extra prompts. 

The END.
